title: sign_verify_roundtrip test uses wolfTPM as its own oracle
description: wolftpm/src/key.rs test sign_verify_roundtrip signs with EccKey::sign and verifies with EccKey::verify against the same key. Per CLAUDE.md test integrity rules: 'A test that encrypts with function A and decrypts with function A proves nothing.' A bug in the sign/verify FFI binding (e.g. wrong algorithm constant, transposed sig and hash arguments) would not be caught. The test was pre-existing before 4797181 but it remains present and uncorrected. Independent oracle required: e.g. verify the signature with ring::signature::UnparsedPublicKey or openssl, or use a known P-256 ECDSA test vector.
